description_long: This is a large, twin-engine airplane with a high-mounted, straight wing configuration and a robust fuselage. The aircraft features two visible propeller engines mounted on the leading edge of the wings, and a distinctive tail section with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two large floats, indicating it is a seaplane designed for water landings and takeoffs. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a streamlined nose section. The overall surface is smooth and metallic, with minimal visible markings or color variation.
description: A large, twin-propeller seaplane with high-mounted straight wings, dual floats, and a streamlined, enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wings, twin engines, and the full length of the fuselage with the tail and floats visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, dual propeller engines, and the two large floats beneath the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage, wings, and tail assembly.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the alignment of the floats under the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two large main floats for water operations.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit at the front of the fuselage, likely multi-seat.
